name: Maitreya
shortDescription: Maitreya is the future Buddha, prophesied to appear on Earth in the future, achieve complete enlightenment, and teach the pure dharma. He represents the hope for universal enlightenment and the continuation of the Buddhist teachings.
description: Maitreya is the future Buddha, a bodhisattva who will appear on Earth in the future, achieve complete enlightenment, and teach the pure dharma. According to Buddhist tradition, Maitreya is currently residing in Tushita Heaven, awaiting the opportune time for his descent. He is a central figure in many Buddhist schools, particularly those emphasizing the importance of future hope and the continuation of the Buddhist path. The prophecy of Maitreya's arrival provides solace and inspiration to Buddhists, assuring them that even in times of suffering and decline, the possibility of enlightenment remains. Maitreya's teachings are expected to be more accessible and widespread than those of Gautama Buddha, leading to a golden age of dharma. Iconographically, Maitreya is often depicted seated on a throne, signifying his future role as a teaching Buddha, and sometimes holding a water flask, representing the potential for healing and purification. He is also associated with the concept of universal loving-kindness and compassion, embodying the ideal of a bodhisattva who postpones their own nirvana to help all beings achieve enlightenment. The belief in Maitreya's future arrival encourages Buddhists to cultivate virtuous qualities and practice diligently, in preparation for the time when they can receive his teachings directly. Some Buddhist traditions also emphasize the importance of creating a conducive environment for Maitreya's appearance through ethical conduct and social harmony.

sources.title: The Princeton Dictionary of Buddhism
sources.author: Robert E. Buswell Jr. and Donald S. Lopez Jr.

sources.title: In the Image of Tibet: Tibetan Painting After 1950
sources.author: Clare Harris

worshipPractices.name: Meditation on Loving-Kindness
worshipPractices.description: Devotees cultivate loving-kindness (metta) towards all beings, aspiring to embody Maitreya's compassionate nature. This practice aims to purify the mind and prepare oneself for Maitreya's future arrival and teachings. It often involves chanting or visualizing Maitreya.

worshipPractices.name: Making Offerings
worshipPractices.description: Offerings such as flowers, incense, and food are made to images or representations of Maitreya. These offerings symbolize devotion and a desire to connect with Maitreya's benevolent qualities. The act of offering is believed to accumulate merit and bring blessings.

festivals.name: Vesak (Buddha Day)
festivals.description: While primarily celebrating the birth, enlightenment, and death of Gautama Buddha, Vesak often includes special prayers and ceremonies dedicated to Maitreya. Some traditions focus on the anticipation of Maitreya's future arrival and the hope for a more enlightened world.

festivals.name: Losar (Tibetan New Year)
festivals.description: During Losar, prayers are offered for the swift return of Maitreya and for the flourishing of the Dharma in the future. The festival marks a time of renewal and optimism, aligned with the hope and compassion associated with Maitreya.

cultural.modernLegacy: Maitreya continues to inspire hope for a better future and serves as a symbol of universal loving-kindness. Representations of Maitreya, particularly the 'Laughing Buddha' form (often mistakenly associated with Maitreya but inspired by a Chinese monk considered his emanation), are prevalent in art and popular culture, embodying prosperity and joy. The concept of Maitreya influences contemporary Buddhist movements focused on social justice and creating a more compassionate world.

cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: Kalki is the final avatar of Vishnu, prophesied to appear at the end of Kali Yuga to restore dharma and usher in a new age, similar to Maitreya's role as a future Buddha who will teach the pure dharma.

Saoshyant is a future savior figure who will appear at the end of time to defeat evil and bring about the final renovation of the world, much like Maitreya's future arrival to guide humanity towards enlightenment.

The Second Coming of Jesus Christ involves his return to Earth to judge the living and the dead, and to establish a new kingdom of peace and righteousness, mirroring Maitreya's role in ushering in a new era of enlightenment and dharma.

The Messiah is a future king and leader who will usher in an era of peace, justice, and spiritual renewal for the Jewish people and the world, reflecting Maitreya's role as a future Buddha who will guide humanity towards enlightenment.

associations.name: Future
associations.significance: Maitreya is the Buddha of the future, representing hope and the promise of enlightenment in a later age.

associations.name: Tushita Heaven
associations.significance: Maitreya currently resides in Tushita Heaven, a realm of joy and contentment, awaiting his descent to Earth.

associations.name: Golden
associations.significance: Golden symbolizes enlightenment, purity, and the radiant qualities associated with Maitreya's future teachings.

associations.name: Compassion
associations.significance: As a Bodhisattva, Maitreya embodies boundless compassion for all beings, delaying his own enlightenment to help others.

associations.name: Lotus
associations.significance: The lotus symbolizes purity, spiritual awakening, and the unfolding of enlightenment, mirroring Maitreya's future Buddhahood.

associations.name: 5.67 Billion
associations.significance: This is approximately the number of years that, according to some Buddhist traditions, must pass before Maitreya appears on Earth.

associations.name: Dharma
associations.significance: Maitreya's primary purpose upon his arrival is to teach the pure Dharma, guiding humanity towards enlightenment.

companions.name: Ajita
companions.description: Ajita is one of Maitreya's foremost disciples and is often depicted alongside him. He is considered a future Buddha himself.

companions.name: Tushita Devas
companions.description: As Maitreya resides in Tushita Heaven, he is surrounded by the devas (gods) of that realm. They are his attendants and companions in this celestial abode.

companions.name: King Cakkavatti
companions.description: King Cakkavatti is a righteous ruler who will appear during Maitreya's time. He will be a supporter of Maitreya's teachings and a righteous king.

companions.name: Arya Asanga
companions.description: Arya Asanga is said to have visited Maitreya in Tushita Heaven and received teachings from him. This helped propagate the Yogacara school of Buddhism.

extendedContent.title: Origins and Prophecies of Maitreya
extendedContent.content:
The concept of Maitreya as the future Buddha arises from the fundamental Buddhist belief in the cyclical nature of time and the continuous appearance of Buddhas to guide beings towards enlightenment. Unlike Gautama Buddha, who attained enlightenment in our present era, Maitreya is prophesied to appear in a future epoch when the Dharma, the teachings of Gautama Buddha, have largely been forgotten. The prophecy details a world vastly different from our own, a time of peace, prosperity, and righteousness where the conditions are ripe for Maitreya's teaching. The Cakkavatti-Sihanada Sutta of the Digha Nikaya contains one of the earliest depictions of this future world, describing a time of abundance and ethical conduct, setting the stage for Maitreya's arrival.

According to tradition, Maitreya is currently residing in the Tushita Heaven (Tusita), one of the six heavens of the desire realm. He is said to be awaiting the right time to be reborn on Earth and initiate a new cycle of Dharma. The Maitreya-vyakarana, a specific text dedicated to Maitreya's future appearance, elaborates on the signs that will herald his coming, including a decrease in negative karmas and an increase in positive actions among beings. While in Tushita, Maitreya teaches the Dharma to the devas (gods) and bodhisattvas residing there, preparing himself for his eventual descent and the propagation of the Dharma in the world.

Several Mahayana sutras, such as the Lotus Sutra and the Avatamsaka Sutra, also mention Maitreya, emphasizing his bodhisattva vows and his commitment to helping all beings attain enlightenment. These texts highlight the importance of cultivating faith and devotion to Maitreya as a means of accumulating merit and ensuring a favorable rebirth in the future era when he will appear. The hope for Maitreya's arrival serves as a powerful motivator for Buddhist practitioners to uphold ethical conduct, cultivate wisdom, and practice compassion, thereby contributing to the creation of a more peaceful and harmonious world that will welcome the future Buddha.

extendedContent.title: Iconography and Representation of Maitreya
extendedContent.content:
Maitreya's iconography varies across different Buddhist traditions and time periods, but certain key features are consistently associated with him. Often, he is depicted as a princely bodhisattva, adorned with jewels and wearing a crown, reflecting his status as a future Chakravartin (universal monarch) and his residence in Tushita Heaven. He may also be shown seated on a throne or chair, signifying his imminent arrival and his role as a teacher. In contrast to the typical seated posture of Gautama Buddha, Maitreya is frequently depicted seated with his legs crossed at the ankles, ready to rise and descend to Earth.

Another common representation of Maitreya is the 'Laughing Buddha' or 'Happy Buddha,' particularly prevalent in Chinese and East Asian Buddhism. This figure, often depicted as a portly, smiling monk carrying a sack, is believed to be an incarnation of Maitreya. While not directly mentioned in the earliest Buddhist texts, this popular image embodies the joy, compassion, and abundance associated with Maitreya's future reign. The sack he carries is often interpreted as a symbol of wealth, happiness, and the ability to relieve suffering.

Furthermore, Maitreya is sometimes represented holding a water pot, symbolizing the nectar of immortality and the Dharma that will quench the thirst of all beings. He may also hold a wheel (chakra), representing the turning of the Dharma wheel and the spread of his teachings. In some traditions, a stupa (a Buddhist reliquary) is depicted in his headdress, symbolizing his connection to the Buddha's teachings and the sacred nature of enlightenment. The visual representations of Maitreya serve not only as objects of veneration but also as reminders of the potential for future happiness and enlightenment, encouraging practitioners to cultivate virtuous qualities and prepare for the arrival of the future Buddha.

extendedContent.title: Worship and Temples Dedicated to Maitreya
extendedContent.content:
Devotion to Maitreya is widespread throughout the Buddhist world, with numerous temples and monasteries dedicated to his worship. In many Buddhist traditions, chanting Maitreya's name and reciting mantras associated with him are considered powerful practices for accumulating merit and ensuring a favorable rebirth in his future era. The Maitreya Sutra, which details his future appearance and teachings, is often recited and studied by devotees seeking to connect with him.

In countries like China, Korea, and Japan, temples dedicated to the 'Laughing Buddha' (Hotei in Japan, Budai in China), who is regarded as an emanation of Maitreya, are common. These temples are often adorned with statues of the jovial monk, and devotees offer prayers and make offerings in hopes of receiving blessings of good fortune and happiness. The image of the Laughing Buddha is also frequently displayed in homes and businesses as a symbol of prosperity and well-being.

Furthermore, in some Tibetan Buddhist traditions, specific practices and rituals are performed to invoke Maitreya's blessings and to purify negative karma. The Gelug school of Tibetan Buddhism, in particular, emphasizes the importance of Maitreya's teachings and the cultivation of bodhicitta (the aspiration to attain enlightenment for the benefit of all beings), which is seen as a key preparation for his future appearance. The construction of large statues of Maitreya, such as the Maitreya Project in Kushinagar, India, demonstrates the continued devotion and aspiration to connect with the future Buddha and to create a world conducive to his teachings.

extendedContent.title: Maitreya's Role in the Cosmic Order and Buddhist Eschatology
extendedContent.content:
Maitreya's role extends beyond simply being a future Buddha; he embodies a crucial aspect of Buddhist eschatology and the cyclical nature of Dharma's presence in the world. His anticipated arrival addresses the inherent impermanence of all phenomena, including the teachings of Gautama Buddha. When the Dharma fades from the world, Maitreya's appearance signals a renewal, a fresh start for beings to reconnect with the path to liberation. This cyclical view of Dharma underscores the importance of preserving and propagating the teachings, as well as preparing oneself for the future when Maitreya will appear.

The concept of Maitreya also serves as a counterbalance to potential despair or fatalism. Even in times of great suffering and moral decay, the prophecy of Maitreya offers hope and reassurance that enlightenment is always attainable. The emphasis on his future appearance motivates practitioners to engage in virtuous actions and cultivate positive qualities, contributing to the creation of a more harmonious and enlightened world. It reinforces the Buddhist principle of karma, suggesting that individual actions can collectively influence the future and pave the way for Maitreya's coming.

Moreover, Maitreya's role in the cosmic order is linked to the concept of bodhisattvas and their vows to postpone their own final nirvana to help other beings. By residing in Tushita Heaven and awaiting the opportune moment to descend, Maitreya exemplifies the bodhisattva ideal of selfless service and unwavering commitment to the liberation of all sentient beings. He serves as an inspiration for practitioners to embody the qualities of compassion, wisdom, and skillful means in their own lives, thereby contributing to the overall well-being of the world and hastening the arrival of a more enlightened future. His appearance marks not just a new Buddha, but a new era of Dharma accessibility for all.
